provenance: Hand-computed. Bailey & López de Prado 2014, 'The Deflated Sharpe Ratio: Correcting for Selection Bias, Backtest Overfitting, and Non-Normality', Financial Analysts Journal, Eq. 9. Formula: DSR = (SR_obs - SR_0) * sqrt(T-1) / sqrt(1 - gamma3*SR_obs + (gamma4-1)/4 * SR_obs^2). SR_0 = 0 (null hypothesis). Independently verified in Python.
description: Fat-tailed / leptokurtic returns: gamma3=0 (no skew), gamma4=5.0 (strong excess kurtosis). SR_obs=0.8, T=126 (half-year), SR_0=0. Fat tails (gamma4>3) increase the denominator, deflating DSR — strategies with fat-tailed return distributions overstate their Sharpe reliability. Shorter T=126 tests the sqrt(T-1) scaling separately from the 252-day cases.
